title: GSAT-7 Series Military Satellites
focus: Dedicated military communication and surveillance satellites for the Indian Armed Forces.
rationale: A brief but highly testable matching-type topic regarding which satellite serves which branch of the Armed Forces.
sectionExcerpt: Details the GSAT-7 series of military satellites: GSAT-7 (Rukmini) for the Navy, GSAT-7A (Angry Bird) and upcoming GSAT-7C for the Air Force, and GSAT-7B for the Army to enhance border surveillance and communication.
evidenceBullets: GSAT-7 (Rukmini) is India's first officially dedicated military satellite, used by the Navy.
GSAT-7A (Angry Bird) helps the IAF connect platforms like AWACS, UAVs, and aircraft.
GSAT-7B is being developed for the Army to enhance surveillance and communication in border areas.
GSAT-7C is being developed for the Air Force and includes ground hubs for real-time, secure communication.

title: Mk-II (A) Laser-Directed Energy Weapon (DEW) System
focus: DRDO's indigenous laser weapon system, DEW technology concepts, and global DEW examples.
rationale: High-yield tech topic covering both the specific DRDO system and the general science behind Directed Energy Weapons, plus international equivalents.
sectionExcerpt: DRDO successfully trialed the Mk-II (A) LASER-DEW system at NOAR, Andhra Pradesh. Developed by CHESS, Hyderabad, it uses a 30-kilowatt laser beam. The section also covers the science of DEWs (using electromagnetic/particle energy instead of kinetic), their limitations in bad weather, and global systems like Israel's Iron Beam, USA's HELIOS, and Russia's Peresvet.
evidenceBullets: Mk-II (A) DEW was developed indigenously by DRDO's Centre for High Energy Systems and Sciences (CHESS), Hyderabad.
It uses an intense high-energy 30-kilowatt LASER beam to engage targets detected by radar or Electro Optic systems.
DEWs use concentrated electromagnetic or particle energy rather than kinetic energy, but are less effective in weather conditions like fog and storms.
Israel is the first country to shoot down enemy drones with a laser weapon using its Iron Beam system.
Global DEW systems include USA's HELIOS, China's Silent Hunter, UK's DragonFire, and Russia's Peresvet.

title: Stratospheric Airship Platform (SAP)
focus: DRDO's high-altitude pseudo-satellite (HAPS) technology for ISR capabilities.
rationale: Emerging technology in aerospace (HAPS). Good potential for questions on its operational altitude, lift mechanism, and strategic advantages over traditional satellites.
sectionExcerpt: DRDO conducted maiden flight-trials of the Stratospheric Airship Platform (SAP), developed by ADRDE, Agra. SAP is an unmanned High-Altitude Pseudo-Satellite (HAPS) operating in the stratosphere (17-22 km). It uses helium for buoyancy and solar energy for power, bridging the gap between drones and satellites for ISR and border monitoring.
evidenceBullets: SAP was developed by the Aerial Delivery Research and Development Establishment (ADRDE), Agra under DRDO.
It operates in the stratosphere, typically between 17 to 22 kilometers above Earth.
Functions as a High-Altitude Pseudo-Satellite (HAPS), using helium for buoyancy and solar energy with onboard batteries for power.
HAPS bridges the gap between drones and satellites, offering space-like coverage with faster deployment and lower costs.

title: Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) and Fighter Jet Generations
focus: India's 5th generation fighter jet programme and the evolution of fighter jet generations.
rationale: Important defense manufacturing topic. Questions can target the AMCA programme details, features of 5th Gen fighters, and the distinction between 4th, 4.5th, and 5th generation aircraft.
sectionExcerpt: India is jointly manufacturing advanced jet engines for its 5th generation Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) with French aerospace major Safran. Led by ADA under DRDO, AMCA features supercruise, stealth, and AI avionics. The section also outlines the features of 5th generation fighters (twin-engine, stealth) and compares them with 4th (MiG-29, F-16) and 4.5th (Rafale) generation jets.
evidenceBullets: India is partnering with French aerospace major Safran to manufacture advanced jet engines for the AMCA programme.
The AMCA programme is led by the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) under DRDO.
Only the US (F-22, F-35), Russia (Su-57), and China (J-20) currently have operational 5th generation aircraft.
5th generation fighters feature stealth capabilities, twin engines, supercruise, and internal weapons bays.
4.5th generation fighters (e.g., Rafale, Eurofighter Typhoon) added stealth and radar absorbent materials to extend 4th generation capabilities.
